key_insight: A hybrid DFT + machine-learning workflow can rapidly screen thousands of equimolar quinary NaCl-type entropy-stabilized oxide compositions and identify the most promising stable candidates.
engineering_relevance: This provides a practical route to reduce expensive first-principles calculations while accelerating materials discovery for multicomponent oxides.
method: Built a reference database of binary and ternary oxides using GGA/meta-GGA DFT.
Represented quinary compositions with SQS supercells and convex-hull thermodynamics.
Trained supervised models to predict distance to hull and stabilization temperature across 4368 compositions.
evidence_signal: An optimized multilayer perceptron achieved the best performance, with about 4 kJ/mol test error, while requiring explicit DFT for only around 10% of the quinary systems.
watch_item: Predicted stabilization temperatures still depend on thermodynamic approximations, so absolute values may be systematically biased even when trends are captured.
paper_title: Data-Driven Prediction of NaCl-Type Entropy-Stabilized Oxide Compositions from First-Principles and Supervised Learning
